structured_payload_candidate.title: Dual-Duct VAV Endpoints by Zone Group Mode

structured_payload_candidate.summary: Active endpoints for cooling maximum, minimum, and heating maximum airflow setpoints vary based on the Zone Group mode as defined in Table 5.11.4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone Group mode changes (Occupied, Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, Unoccupied).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set cooling maximum, minimum, and heating maximum endpoints according to Table 5.11.4.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Active endpoints used in the control logic depicted in Figures 5.11. 5-1 and 5.11. 5-2 shall vary depending on the mode  of the Zone Group  the zone is a part of  (see Table 5.11.4).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dual-Duct VAV Endpoints Table
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit − Snap-Acting Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Table 5.11.4 specifies the active endpoints for cooling maximum, minimum, and heating maximum airflow setpoints for each Zone Group mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone Group mode is Occupied, Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, or Unoccupied.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply the corresponding endpoint values from the table.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Table 5.11.4 Endpoints  as a Function of Zone Group  Mode
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
Heating maximum  Vheat -max 0 0 Vheat -max Vheat -max 0

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dual-Duct VAV Cooling Mode with Dual Inlet Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with dual inlet airflow sensors
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling-loop output resets the active cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum endpoint. The cold duct damper modulates to maintain measured cooling airflow at setpoint, and the hot duct damper is closed. If cold-deck supply air temperature exceeds room temperature, the cold duct airflow setpoint is limited to the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint based on cooling-loop output. Modulate cold duct damper to maintain measured cooling airflow at setpoint. Close hot duct damper. If cold-deck SAT > room temperature, limit cold duct airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall reset the active cold duct  airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint . The cold duct  damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cooling airflow at the active cold duct airflow setpoint . The hot duct  damper shall be closed.
1. If cold -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active cold duct airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

evidence_quote:
a. When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall reset the active cold duct  airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint . The cold duct  damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cooling airflow at the active cold duct airflow setpoint . The hot duct  damper shall be closed.
1. If cold -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active cold duct airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .
evidence_text:
a. When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall reset the active cold duct  airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint . The cold duct  damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cooling airflow at the active cold duct airflow setpoint . The hot duct  damper shall be closed.
1. If cold -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active cold duct airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dual-Duct VAV Deadband Mode with Dual Inlet Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with dual inlet airflow sensors
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband mode, the active cold duct and hot duct airflow setpoints are held at their last values before entering deadband. When transitioning from cooling to deadband, the cold duct airflow setpoint equals the minimum endpoint and hot duct airflow setpoint is zero. When transitioning from heating to deadband, the hot duct airflow setpoint equals the minimum endpoint and cold duct airflow setpoint is zero. This results in a snap-action switch.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Hold last airflow setpoints. From cooling to deadband: cold duct setpoint = minimum endpoint, hot duct setpoint = 0. From heating to deadband: hot duct setpoint = minimum endpoint, cold duct setpoint = 0.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. When the Zone State  is deadband , the active cold duct and hot duct  airflow setpoint s shall be their last setpoint s just before entering deadband.  In other words, when going from cooling  to deadband , the active cold duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the active hot duct airflow setpoint  is zero. When going from heating  to deadband , the active hot duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the active cold duct airflow setpoint  is zero. This results in a snap -action switch in the damper setpoint  as indicated in Figures 5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.

evidence_quote: b. When the Zone State  is deadband , the active cold duct and hot duct  airflow setpoint s shall be their last setpoint s just before entering deadband.  In other words, when going from cooling  to deadband , the active cold duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the active hot duct airflow setpoint  is zero. When going from heating  to deadband , the active hot duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the active cold duct airflow setpoint  is zero. This results in a snap -action switch in the damper setpoint  as indicated in Figures 5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.
evidence_text: b. When the Zone State  is deadband , the active cold duct and hot duct  airflow setpoint s shall be their last setpoint s just before entering deadband.  In other words, when going from cooling  to deadband , the active cold duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the active hot duct airflow setpoint  is zero. When going from heating  to deadband , the active hot duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the active cold duct airflow setpoint  is zero. This results in a snap -action switch in the damper setpoint  as indicated in Figures 5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.

structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors

The hot duct damper modulates to maintain measured heating airflow at setpoint, and the cold duct damper is closed. If hot-deck supply air temperature is less than room temperature, the hot duct airflow setpoint is limited to the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset hot duct airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint based on heating-loop output. Modulate hot duct damper to maintain measured heating airflow at setpoint. Close cold duct damper. If hot-deck SAT < room temperature, limit hot duct airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint.

c. When the Zone State  is heati ng, the heating -loop output shall reset the active hot duct  airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint . The hot duct  damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured heating airflow at the active hot duct airflow setpoint . The cold duct  damper shall be closed.
1. If hot -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is less than room temperature, the active hot duct  airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

evidence_quote:
c. When the Zone State  is heati ng, the heating -loop output shall reset the active hot duct  airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint . 1. If hot -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is less than room temperature, the active hot duct  airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dual-Duct VAV Deadband Mode with Single Discharge Sensor
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Temperature and Damper Control with a Single Discharge Airflow Sensor
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with single discharge airflow sensor
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband mode, the active discharge airflow setpoint is the minimum endpoint, maintained by the damper that was operative just before entering deadband. The other damper remains closed. From cooling to deadband, the cold duct damper maintains discharge airflow at minimum endpoint and hot duct damper is closed. From heating to deadband, the hot duct damper maintains discharge airflow at minimum endpoint and cold duct damper is closed. This results in a snap-action switch.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set discharge airflow setpoint to minimum endpoint. Maintain with last operative damper; close other damper. From cooling: cold duct damper active, hot duct closed. From heating: hot duct damper active, cold duct closed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. When the Zone State  is deadband , the active discharge airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint , maintained by the damper that was operative just before entering deadband.  The other damper shall remain closed. In other words, when going from cooling  to deadband , the cold duct damper shall maintain the discharge airflow at the minimum endpoint , and the hot duct damper shall be closed. When going from heating  to deadband , the hot duct damper shall maintain the discharge airflow at the zone minimum endpoint , and the cold duct damper shall be closed. This results in a snap -action switch in the damp er setpoint  as indicated in Figures 5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.

evidence_quote: b. When the Zone State  is deadband , the active discharge airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint , maintained by the damper that was operative just before entering deadband.  The other damper shall remain closed. In other words, when going from cooling  to deadband , the cold duct damper shall maintain the discharge airflow at the minimum endpoint , and the hot duct damper shall be closed. When going from heating  to deadband , the hot duct damper shall maintain the discharge airflow at the zone minimum endpoint , and the cold duct damper shall be closed. This results in a snap -action switch in the damp er setpoint  as indicated in Figures 5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.
evidence_text: b. When the Zone State  is deadband , the active discharge airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint , maintained by the damper that was operative just before entering deadband.  The other damper shall remain closed. In other words, when going from cooling  to deadband , the cold duct damper shall maintain the discharge airflow at the minimum endpoint , and the hot duct damper shall be closed. When going from heating  to deadband , the hot duct damper shall maintain the discharge airflow at the zone minimum endpoint , and the cold duct damper shall be closed. This results in a snap -action switch in the damp er setpoint  as indicated in Figures 5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dual-Duct VAV Override to Prevent Backflow
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Overriding Sections 5.11.5.1 and 5.11.5.2 Logic (to Avoid Backflow from One Duct to the Other)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed. If the cooling air handler is not proven ON, the cooling damper shall be closed.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Heating air handler not proven ON, or cooling air handler not proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Close heating damper if heating AHU not proven ON. Close cooling damper if cooling AHU not proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. If heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed.
b. If cooling air handler is not proven ON, the cooling damper shall be closed.

evidence_quote:
a. If heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed.
b. If cooling air handler is not proven ON, the cooling damper shall be closed.
evidence_text:
a. If heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed.
b. If cooling air handler is not proven ON, the cooling damper shall be closed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Airflow Alarm Level 4
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm if measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes and setpoint > 0.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Airflow Alarm Suppression for Zones with Importance-Multiplier 0
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Suppress low airflow alarms for zones with an Importance-Multiplier of 0 for their static pressure reset T&R control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone has Importance-Multiplier = 0 for static pressure reset T&R control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress low airflow alarms for that zone.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 3 alarm if the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fan off and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of maximum airflow setpoint, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Fan off and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of maximum airflow setpoint, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor readin g is above the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_text: If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor readin g is above the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Damper Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm if damper position is 0% and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the damper is proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Damper position = 0% and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of cooling maximum airflow setpoint, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes while fan is proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Damper position = 0% and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of cooling maximum airflow setpoint, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes while fan is proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0 %, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the damper is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Override zone airflow setpoint or damper positions as specified.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Force zone airflow setpoint to zero, Vcool-max, Vmin, Vheat-max; force cooling damper full closed/open; force heating damper full closed/open; reset request-hours accumulator point to zero.

e. force  cooling damper full closed/open ,
f. force  heating damper full closed/open , and
g. reset  request -hours accumulator point to ze ro (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section ).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests for Dual-Duct VAV
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send cooling SAT reset requests based on zone temperature exceeding cooling setpoint by certain thresholds or cooling loop output exceeding 95%.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes after suppression period, or by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes after suppression period, or Cooling Loop > 95%.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send 3 requests if zone temp > cooling setpoint + 3°C (5°F) for 2 min; send 2 requests if zone temp > cooling setpoint + 2°C (3°F) for 2 min; send 1 request if Cooling Loop > 95% until Cooling Loop < 85%; send 0 requests if Cooling Loop < 95%.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Cooling Loop  is less than 85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote:
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Cooling Loop  is less than 85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
evidence_text:
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Cooling Loop  is less than 85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cold-Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests for Dual-Duct VAV
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send cold-duct static pressure reset requests based on measured airflow deficit relative to setpoint and damper position exceeding 95%.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Measured airflow < 50% of setpoint while setpoint > 0 and damper position > 95% for 1 minute, or measured airflow < 70% of setpoint while setpoint > 0 and damper position > 95% for 1 minute, or damper position > 95%.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send 3 requests if airflow < 50% of setpoint and damper > 95% for 1 min; send 2 requests if airflow < 70% of setpoint and damper > 95% for 1 min; send 1 request if damper > 95% until damper < 85%; send 0 requests if damper < 95%.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the mea sured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests.
c. Else if the damper  position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the damper  position is less than 85%.
d. Else if the damper  position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote:
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the mea sured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests.
c. Else if the damper  position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the damper  position is less than 85%.
d. Else if the damper  position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
evidence_text:
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the mea sured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests.
c. Else if the damper  position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the damper  position is less than 85%.
d. Else if the damper  position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
